methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Fortera, a Series C-stage climate and energy company founded by Ryan Gilliam, has raised over $200M to date. The valuation assessment relies on a comparable universe of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy) alongside broader reference to a peer set of eight private firms and one public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies $340M. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. The peer median method reflects material dispersion in valuations among similar-stage climate technology companies, suggesting heterogeneous investor views on technical and commercial risk within the sector.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $289M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $670M. The wide spread—spanning a 3.8x ratio from low to high—reflects genuine uncertainty around Fortera's relative standing within the peer cohort and the early-stage nature of climate tech valuations. Key sensitivities include the strength of Fortera's technology differentiation relative to KoBold and Electric Hydrogen, capital efficiency relative to peers, and near-term commercial traction.
